data.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [T11] Data-migration / backfill safety — VERDICT: NOT-APPLICABLE (PASS, empty findings).

T11 is an overlay that applies only when a plan changes a schema/persisted format or backfills persisted data. This epic's live deliverables (WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING) do none of those:
- New documentation artifact (docs/workflow-engine.md) — a doc file, not a data store.
- New eval spec YAMLs under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) — additive new files.
- New deterministic-scorer registry of pure functions + validate_scorer — in-code, no persisted state.
- Wiring run_eval() to a live Inspect harness (replacing the NotImplementedError stub at src/rebar/llm/eval.py:319/346) — an execution path, not a format change.
- New CI workflows (eval-discipline, eval-live) — config, not data.
- New fixture/dataset corpus + "dataset schema" — these are NEW eval artifacts (gold_set/fixtures), not a migration or backfill of existing persisted production rows.

No expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write, or rollback obligations arise because no existing persisted format is altered and no existing data is backfilled. Per the criterion's ANTI-FP ("not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes"), this PASSES with no finding.

Affirmations (T11): (a) online/expand-contract — n/a; (b) batching/scale — n/a; (c) resumability/idempotency — n/a; (d) dual-write window — n/a; (e) rollback/no-data-loss — n/a. All not-applicable; clean.
